data.title: Microservices
data.description: Netflix uses a microservices architecture to deploy and manage its applications, allowing for scalability and rapid deployment.
data.technical_spec: Each service is independently deployable, managed with containers, and communicates over their own APIs.

data.title: Global Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Utilizes CDNs for efficient content distribution and localization, providing low-latency access to users worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Redundant nodes across global regions ensure high availability and quick loading times.

data.title: Reactive Systems
data.description: Incorporates reactive principles to ensure a responsive, resilient, and elastic user interaction experience.
data.technical_spec: Systems are designed to be event-driven, scalable, and capable of handling unpredictable demand.

data.title: Cloud-Based Infrastructure
data.description: Operates primarily on the cloud, allowing extensive compute power and storage scalability.
data.technical_spec: Uses AWS for hosting and services, facilitating dynamic scaling, storage, and processing.

data.title: Data-Driven Decision Making
data.description: Relies on big data analytics for decision support, personalization, and content recommendations.
data.technical_spec: Integrated data warehouses and analytics engines process massive datasets for customer insights.
